query: Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?

row.rationale: Fluvial sedimentology — direct Earth-analog sedimentary geology
row.paper: Davis, J.M., et al., 2019. A diverse array of fluvial depositional systems in Arabia Terra. JGR Planets, 124(7), pp.1913-1934. DOI:10.1029/2019JE005976

tool_input.summary: To assess whether Arabia Terra deposits reflect a single fluvial style or multiple depositional systems, the most relevant PDS Geosciences-node holdings are (1) regional topography (MGS MOLA gridded/shot data) to map drainage, slopes, basin geometry, and stratigraphic relationships; (2) hyperspectral mineralogy (MRO CRISM map-projected RDRs) to distinguish sedimentary facies/alteration that can indicate different fluvial–lacustrine–alluvial settings; (3) thermophysical properties (MGS TES-derived thermal inertia) to help separate indurated layered deposits vs loose sediment; and (4) shallow radar stratigraphy (MRO SHARAD RDR) to test for buried layering/contacts. These datasets together enable testing for diverse depositional systems vs a single endmember style.

candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES
candidates.reasoning: MOLA Mission Experiment Gridded Data Record (MEGDR) provides regional-to-global topography needed to map valley-network gradients, basin boundaries, longitudinal profiles, and paleo-base levels across Arabia Terra—key for distinguishing, e.g., integrated river systems vs localized alluvial fan/playa systems.

candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER IEGDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: The individual experiment gridded data record (IEGDR) supports more detailed/track-aware topographic analysis than purely global grids, useful where subtle relief controls depositional architecture in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES
candidates.reasoning: The Precision Experiment Data Record (PEDR) is the most detailed MOLA altimetry (shot/track level), enabling targeted checks of terrace/bench elevations, channel long profiles, and subtle topographic breaks that can indicate multiple depositional/erosional episodes.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: CRISM map-projected targeted RDRs (MTRDR) are commonly used to interpret mineral assemblages in layered deposits (clays, sulfates, etc.). Facies/mineral changes across Arabia Terra deposits can help discriminate a single fluvial style from a mix of fluvial, deltaic, lacustrine, and groundwater-related sedimentary systems.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: CRISM multispectral map-projected RDRs (MRDR) provide broader spatial coverage (lower spectral detail than targeted), useful for regional mineralogic mapping across Arabia Terra to test whether deposits share one consistent compositional signature or multiple distinct ones.

candidates.title: MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR TES-DERIVED THERMAL INERTIA
candidates.reasoning: TES-derived thermal inertia maps help distinguish indurated bedrock/lithified layered deposits from unconsolidated sediment and mantling units. Spatial variations in thermophysical properties across Arabia Terra deposits can support interpretations of multiple depositional environments and/or post-depositional cementation histories.

candidates.title: MRO SHARAD OBSERVATIONS
candidates.reasoning: SHARAD RDR radargrams can reveal subsurface layering, buried channels, and stratigraphic discontinuities. In Arabia Terra, subsurface architecture is a strong test for whether deposits represent one consistent fluvial package or multiple stacked/juxtaposed depositional systems.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Geosciences node at https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/.

Directory layout:
  mission/ → dataset_or_bundle/ → volume/ (PDS3) or sub-collections (PDS4)

Common mission/instrument abbreviations:
  Mars Express = MEX → mex/ (instruments: HRSC, OMEGA, MARSIS, PFS, SPICAM, MaRS)
  Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter = MRO → mro/ (instruments: HiRISE, CTX, CRISM, SHARAD, MCS)
  Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity = MSL → msl/ (instruments: ChemCam, APXS, CheMin, SAM, Mastcam, MAHLI, DAN)
  Mars 2020 / Perseverance = M2020 → m2020/ (instruments: PIXL, SHERLOC, Mastcam-Z, SuperCam, RIMFAX)
  Mars Exploration Rovers (Spirit=MER2, Opportunity=MER1) → mer/ (instruments: Pancam, Mini-TES/MTES, APXS, MB, MI)
  Mars Global Surveyor = MGS → mgs/ (instruments: MOC, MOLA, TES, MAG)
  Mars Odyssey = ODY → ody/ (instruments: THEMIS, GRS, NS)
  Phoenix = PHX → phx/ (instruments: TEGA, MECA, SSI, OM, RAC)
  Viking = VL1/VL2/VO1/VO2 → viking/ (instruments: camera, IRTM, MAWD)
  MESSENGER = MESS → messenger/ (instruments: MDIS, GRNS, XRS, MLA, MASCS)
  Magellan = MGN → mgn/ (instruments: SAR, altimetry, radiometry, emissivity)
  LRO → lro/ (instruments: LOLA, Diviner, LROC, Mini-RF, LAMP)
  GRAIL → grail/ (instruments: LGRS)
  NEAR → near/ (instruments: NLR, MSI, XGRS, MAG)

PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

Extracting dataset_id from labels:
  - PDS3: fields.VOLUME.DATA_SET_ID (or top-level DATA_SET_ID)
  - PDS4 bundle: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier
  - PDS4 collection: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools):
  1. pds_geo_list_missions() — returns all 24 mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP. Use this to identify which mission to explore.
  2. pds_geo_list_dataset_dirs(path) — lists sub-directory names under a mission (e.g. path='mex/'). Cheap HTTP, no label parsing. Each dir gets a pds_hint ('PDS3'/'PDS4'/null) from its naming convention. Use this to see what datasets exist, then pick which to probe.
  3. pds_geo_probe_datasets(paths) — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Recurses one level to find leaf nodes. Returns dataset_id, title, pds_version, and slimmed fields. Accepts a list of paths (max 20) so you can batch multiple probes in one call.
  4. pds_geo_inspect_collections(path) — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection*.xml labels. Returns collection-level logical_identifiers. Use ONLY after probe_datasets confirms a PDS4 bundle exists.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires you to return dataset candidates. Queries come from published scientific papers and always imply specific PDS datasets. NEVER dismiss a query as 'interpretation' or 'conceptual'. Always return at least one candidate.

  2. Prefer CALIBRATED and REDUCED data products over raw/EDR when the query implies scientific analysis. Scientists use calibrated (RDR, CDR) or derived (DDR) products. Look for directory names containing 'calibrated', 'rdr', 'cdr', 'ddr'.

  3. For PDS4 datasets, use inspect_collections ONLY for the top 2-3 most relevant bundles. Return the COLLECTION-level LID when a :data or :calibrated collection exists, not just the bundle LID.

  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.

WORKFLOW (follow this order):
  Step 1: Infer the mission(s) and instrument(s) from the query using the abbreviation table above.
  Step 2: Call list_dataset_dirs for the relevant mission directory (e.g. 'mex/'). Scan the directory names and pds_hints to identify the most promising dataset directories.
  Step 3: Call probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch them — up to 20 paths in one call). This returns dataset_id, title, and pds_version for each.
  Step 4: If any probed datasets are PDS4 bundles and you need collection-level LIDs, call inspect_collections on the top 2-3.
  Step 5: Return the candidates with dataset_id, title, and reasoning.

  Skip list_missions if you already know the mission directory from the abbreviation table. Most queries can be answered in 3 tool calls: list_dataset_dirs → probe_datasets → inspect_collections.

Constraints:
  - Stay under 8 tool calls per query. This is a hard limit.
  - Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  - You MUST return at least one candidate for every query.
Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?

results.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER SHADR ARCHIVE

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER SHADR ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: This volume contains the MOLA Spherical Harmonics ASCII Data Record (SHADR).

parts.args: {"summary":"To assess whether Arabia Terra deposits reflect a single fluvial style or multiple depositional systems, the most relevant PDS Geosciences-node holdings are (1) regional topography (MGS MOLA gridded/shot data) to map drainage, slopes, basin geometry, and stratigraphic relationships; (2) hyperspectral mineralogy (MRO CRISM map-projected RDRs) to distinguish sedimentary facies/alteration that can indicate different fluvial–lacustrine–alluvial settings; (3) thermophysical properties (MGS TES-derived thermal inertia) to help separate indurated layered deposits vs loose sediment; and (4) shallow radar stratigraphy (MRO SHARAD RDR) to test for buried layering/contacts. These datasets together enable testing for diverse depositional systems vs a single endmember style.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-mola-5-megdr-l3-v1/","title":"MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES","mission":"MGS","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"MOLA Mission Experiment Gridded Data Record (MEGDR) provides regional-to-global topography needed to map valley-network gradients, basin boundaries, longitudinal profiles, and paleo-base levels across Arabia Terra—key for distinguishing, e.g., integrated river systems vs localized alluvial fan/playa systems."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-IEGDR-L3-V2.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-mola-5-iegdr-l3-v2/","title":"MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER IEGDR ARCHIVE","mission":"MGS","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"The individual experiment gridded data record (IEGDR) supports more detailed/track-aware topographic analysis than purely global grids, useful where subtle relief controls depositional architecture in Arabia Terra."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-3-PEDR-L1A-V1.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-mola-3-pedr-l1a-v1/","title":"MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES","mission":"MGS","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"The Precision Experiment Data Record (PEDR) is the most detailed MOLA altimetry (shot/track level), enabling targeted checks of terrace/bench elevations, channel long profiles, and subtle topographic breaks that can indicate multiple depositional/erosional episodes."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_mtrdr","path":"mro/mro-m-crism-5-rdr-mptargeted-v1/","title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle","mission":"MRO","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"CRISM map-projected targeted RDRs (MTRDR) are commonly used to interpret mineral assemblages in layered deposits (clays, sulfates, etc.). Facies/mineral changes across Arabia Terra deposits can help discriminate a single fluvial style from a mix of fluvial, deltaic, lacustrine, and groundwater-related sedimentary systems."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_mrdr","path":"mro/mro-m-crism-5-rdr-multispectral-v1/","title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MRDR Bundle","mission":"MRO","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"CRISM multispectral map-projected RDRs (MRDR) provide broader spatial coverage (lower spectral detail than targeted), useful for regional mineralogic mapping across Arabia Terra to test whether deposits share one consistent compositional signature or multiple distinct ones."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-TES-5-TIMAP-V1.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-tes-5-timap-v1/","title":"MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR TES-DERIVED THERMAL INERTIA","mission":"MGS","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"TES-derived thermal inertia maps help distinguish indurated bedrock/lithified layered deposits from unconsolidated sediment and mantling units. Spatial variations in thermophysical properties across Arabia Terra deposits can support interpretations of multiple depositional environments and/or post-depositional cementation histories."},{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-SHARAD-4-RDR-V1.0","path":"mro/mro-m-sharad-4-rdr-v1/","title":"MRO SHARAD OBSERVATIONS","mission":"MRO","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"SHARAD RDR radargrams can reveal subsurface layering, buried channels, and stratigraphic discontinuities. In Arabia Terra, subsurface architecture is a strong test for whether deposits represent one consistent fluvial package or multiple stacked/juxtaposed depositional systems."}]}
